column4: Now {{c1::Jethro}}, the priest of Midian, Moses’father-in-law, heard of all that God had done for Moses, and for Israel His people, how that the LORD had brought Israel out of Egypt.
Now Jethro, the priest of {{c1::Midian}}, Moses’father-in-law, heard of all that God had done for Moses, and for Israel His people, how that the LORD had brought Israel out of Egypt.
And {{c1::Jethro}}, Moses’father-in-law, took Zipporah, Moses’wife, after he had sent her away,
And Jethro, {{c1::Moses’father}}-in-law, took Zipporah, Moses’wife, after he had sent her away,
and her two sons; of whom the name of the one was {{c1::Gershom}}; for he said: ‘I have been a stranger in a strange land’;
and her two sons; of whom the name of the one was Gershom; for he said: ‘{{c1::I}} have been a stranger in a strange land’;
and the name of the other was {{c1::Eliezer}}: ‘for the God of my father was my help, and delivered me from the sword of Pharaoh.’
and the name of the other was Eliezer: ‘for the God of my father was my help, and delivered me from the sword of {{c1::Pharaoh}}.’
And {{c1::Jethro}}, Moses’father-in-law, came with his sons and his wife unto Moses into the wilderness where he was encamped, at the mount of God;
And Jethro, {{c1::Moses’father}}-in-law, came with his sons and his wife unto Moses into the wilderness where he was encamped, at the mount of God;
and he said unto {{c1::Moses}}: ‘I thy father-in-law Jethro am coming unto thee, and thy wife, and her two sons with her.’
and he said unto Moses: ‘{{c1::I}} thy father-in-law Jethro am coming unto thee, and thy wife, and her two sons with her.’
And {{c1::Moses}} went out to meet his father-in-law, and bowed down and kissed him; and they asked each other of their welfare; and they came into the tent.
And Moses went out to meet his {{c1::father}}-in-law, and bowed down and kissed him; and they asked each other of their welfare; and they came into the tent.

And {{c1::Moses}} told his father-in-law all that the LORD had done unto Pharaoh and to the Egyptians for Israel’s sake, all the travail that had come upon them by the way, and how the LORD delivered them.

And Moses told his father-in-law all that the LORD had done unto {{c1::Pharaoh}} and to the Egyptians for Israel’s sake, all the travail that had come upon them by the way, and how the LORD delivered them.
And {{c1::Jethro}} rejoiced for all the goodness which the LORD had done to Israel, in that He had delivered them out of the hand of the Egyptians.
And Jethro rejoiced for all the goodness which the LORD had done to {{c1::Israel}}, in that He had delivered them out of the hand of the Egyptians.

And {{c1::Jethro}} said: ‘Blessed be the LORD, who hath delivered you out of the hand of the Egyptians, and out of the hand of Pharaoh; who hath delivered the people from under the hand of the Egyptians.

And Jethro said: ‘{{c1::Blessed}} be the LORD, who hath delivered you out of the hand of the Egyptians, and out of the hand of Pharaoh; who hath delivered the people from under the hand of the Egyptians.
Now {{c1::I}} know that the LORD is greater than all gods; yea, for that they dealt proudly against them.’
Now I know that the LORD is {{c1::greater}} than all gods; yea, for that they dealt proudly against them.’
And {{c1::Jethro}}, Moses’father-in-law, took a burnt-offering and sacrifices for God; and Aaron came, and all the elders of Israel, to eat bread with Moses’father-in-law before God.
And Jethro, {{c1::Moses’father}}-in-law, took a burnt-offering and sacrifices for God; and Aaron came, and all the elders of Israel, to eat bread with Moses’father-in-law before God.
And it came to pass on the morrow, that {{c1::Moses}} sat to judge the people; and the people stood about Moses from the morning unto the evening.
And it came to pass on the {{c1::morrow}}, that Moses sat to judge the people; and the people stood about Moses from the morning unto the evening.

And when {{c1::Moses’father}}-in-law saw all that he did to the people, he said: ‘What is this thing that thou doest to the people? why sittest thou thyself alone, and all the people stand about thee from morning unto even?’

And when Moses’father-in-law saw all that he did to the people, he said: ‘{{c1::What}} is this thing that thou doest to the people? why sittest thou thyself alone, and all the people stand about thee from morning unto even?’
And {{c1::Moses}} said unto his father-in-law: ‘Because the people come unto me to inquire of God;
And Moses said unto his father-in-law: ‘{{c1::Because}} the people come unto me to inquire of God;
when they have a matter, it cometh unto me; and {{c1::I}} judge between a man and his neighbour, and I make them know the statutes of God, and His laws.’
when they have a {{c1::matter}}, it cometh unto me; and I judge between a man and his neighbour, and I make them know the statutes of God, and His laws.’
And {{c1::Moses’father}}-in-law said unto him: ‘The thing that thou doest is not good.
Thou wilt {{c1::surely}} wear away, both thou, and this people that is with thee; for the thing is too heavy for thee; thou art not able to perform it thyself alone.
Thou wilt surely wear away, both thou, and this {{c1::people}} that is with thee; for the thing is too heavy for thee; thou art not able to perform it thyself alone.
Hearken now unto my voice, {{c1::I}} will give thee counsel, and God be with thee: be thou for the people before God, and bring thou the causes unto God.
{{c1::Hearken}} now unto my voice, I will give thee counsel, and God be with thee: be thou for the people before God, and bring thou the causes unto God.
And thou shalt teach them the {{c1::statutes}} and the laws, and shalt show them the way wherein they must walk, and the work that they must do.
And thou shalt teach them the statutes and the laws, and shalt show them the way {{c1::wherein}} they must walk, and the work that they must do.

{{c1::Moreover}} thou shalt provide out of all the people able men, such as fear God, men of truth, hating unjust gain; and place such over them, to be rulers of thousands, rulers of hundreds, rulers of fifties, and rulers of tens.

Moreover thou shalt {{c1::provide}} out of all the people able men, such as fear God, men of truth, hating unjust gain; and place such over them, to be rulers of thousands, rulers of hundreds, rulers of fifties, and rulers of tens.

And let them judge the {{c1::people}} at all seasons; and it shall be, that every great matter they shall bring unto thee, but every small matter they shall judge themselves; so shall they make it easier for thee and bear the burden with thee.

And let them judge the people at all {{c1::seasons}}; and it shall be, that every great matter they shall bring unto thee, but every small matter they shall judge themselves; so shall they make it easier for thee and bear the burden with thee.
If thou shalt do this thing, and God {{c1::command}} thee so, then thou shalt be able to endure, and all this people also shall go to their place in peace.’
If thou shalt do this thing, and God command thee so, then thou shalt be able to {{c1::endure}}, and all this people also shall go to their place in peace.’
So {{c1::Moses}} hearkened to the voice of his father-in-law, and did all that he had said.
So Moses {{c1::hearkened}} to the voice of his father-in-law, and did all that he had said.
And {{c1::Moses}} chose able men out of all Israel, and made them heads over the people, rulers of thousands, rulers of hundreds, rulers of fifties, and rulers of tens.
And Moses chose able men out of all {{c1::Israel}}, and made them heads over the people, rulers of thousands, rulers of hundreds, rulers of fifties, and rulers of tens.
And they judged the people at all seasons: the hard causes they brought unto {{c1::Moses}}, but every small matter they judged themselves.
And they {{c1::judged}} the people at all seasons: the hard causes they brought unto Moses, but every small matter they judged themselves.
And {{c1::Moses}} let his father-in-law depart; and he went his way into his own land.
And Moses let his {{c1::father}}-in-law depart; and he went his way into his own land.
In the third month after the children of {{c1::Israel}} were gone forth out of the land of Egypt, the same day came they into the wilderness of Sinai.
In the third month after the children of Israel were gone forth out of the land of {{c1::Egypt}}, the same day came they into the wilderness of Sinai.
And when they were departed from {{c1::Rephidim}}, and were come to the wilderness of Sinai, they encamped in the wilderness; and there Israel encamped before the mount.
And when they were departed from Rephidim, and were come to the wilderness of {{c1::Sinai}}, they encamped in the wilderness; and there Israel encamped before the mount.
And {{c1::Moses}} went up unto God, and the LORD called unto him out of the mountain, saying: ‘Thus shalt thou say to the house of Jacob, and tell the children of Israel:
And Moses went up unto God, and the LORD called unto him out of the mountain, saying: ‘{{c1::Thus}} shalt thou say to the house of Jacob, and tell the children of Israel:
Ye have seen what {{c1::I}} did unto the Egyptians, and how I bore you on eagles’wings, and brought you unto Myself.
Ye have seen what I did unto the {{c1::Egyptians}}, and how I bore you on eagles’wings, and brought you unto Myself.
Now therefore, if ye will hearken unto {{c1::My}} voice indeed, and keep My covenant, then ye shall be Mine own treasure from among all peoples; for all the earth is Mine;
Now therefore, if ye will hearken unto My voice indeed, and keep My covenant, then ye shall be {{c1::Mine}} own treasure from among all peoples; for all the earth is Mine;
and ye shall be unto {{c1::Me}} a kingdom of priests, and a holy nation. These are the words which thou shalt speak unto the children of Israel.’
and ye shall be unto Me a kingdom of priests, and a holy nation. {{c1::These}} are the words which thou shalt speak unto the children of Israel.’
And {{c1::Moses}} came and called for the elders of the people, and set before them all these words which the LORD commanded him.
And Moses came and {{c1::called}} for the elders of the people, and set before them all these words which the LORD commanded him.
And all the people answered together, and said: ‘{{c1::All}} that the LORD hath spoken we will do.’ And Moses reported the words of the people unto the LORD.
And all the people answered together, and said: ‘All that the LORD hath spoken we will do.’ And {{c1::Moses}} reported the words of the people unto the LORD.

And the LORD said unto {{c1::Moses}}: ‘Lo, I come unto thee in a thick cloud, that the people may hear when I speak with thee, and may also believe thee for ever.’ And Moses told the words of the people unto the LORD.

And the LORD said unto Moses: ‘{{c1::Lo}}, I come unto thee in a thick cloud, that the people may hear when I speak with thee, and may also believe thee for ever.’ And Moses told the words of the people unto the LORD.
And the LORD said unto {{c1::Moses}}: ‘Go unto the people, and sanctify them to-day and to-morrow, and let them wash their garments,
And the LORD said unto Moses: ‘{{c1::Go}} unto the people, and sanctify them to-day and to-morrow, and let them wash their garments,
and be ready against the third day; for the third day the LORD will come down in the sight of all the people upon mount {{c1::Sinai}}.
and be ready {{c1::against}} the third day; for the third day the LORD will come down in the sight of all the people upon mount Sinai.

And thou shalt set bounds unto the people round about, saying: {{c1::Take}} heed to yourselves, that ye go not up into the mount, or touch the border of it; whosoever toucheth the mount shall be surely put to death;

And thou shalt set {{c1::bounds}} unto the people round about, saying: Take heed to yourselves, that ye go not up into the mount, or touch the border of it; whosoever toucheth the mount shall be surely put to death;
no hand shall touch him, but he shall {{c1::surely}} be stoned, or shot through; whether it be beast or man, it shall not live; when the ram’s horn soundeth long, they shall come up to the mount.’
no hand shall touch him, but he shall surely be {{c1::stoned}}, or shot through; whether it be beast or man, it shall not live; when the ram’s horn soundeth long, they shall come up to the mount.’
And {{c1::Moses}} went down from the mount unto the people, and sanctified the people; and they washed their garments.
And Moses went down from the mount unto the {{c1::people}}, and sanctified the people; and they washed their garments.
And he said unto the {{c1::people}}: ‘Be ready against the third day; come not near a woman.’
And he said unto the people: ‘Be ready {{c1::against}} the third day; come not near a woman.’

And it came to pass on the third day, when it was {{c1::morning}}, that there were thunders and lightnings and a thick cloud upon the mount, and the voice of a horn exceeding loud; and all the people that were in the camp trembled.

And it came to pass on the third day, when it was morning, that there were {{c1::thunders}} and lightnings and a thick cloud upon the mount, and the voice of a horn exceeding loud; and all the people that were in the camp trembled.
And {{c1::Moses}} brought forth the people out of the camp to meet God; and they stood at the nether part of the mount.
And Moses {{c1::brought}} forth the people out of the camp to meet God; and they stood at the nether part of the mount.
Now mount {{c1::Sinai}} was altogether on smoke, because the LORD descended upon it in fire; and the smoke thereof ascended as the smoke of a furnace, and the whole mount quaked greatly.
Now mount Sinai was {{c1::altogether}} on smoke, because the LORD descended upon it in fire; and the smoke thereof ascended as the smoke of a furnace, and the whole mount quaked greatly.
And when the voice of the horn waxed louder and louder, {{c1::Moses}} spoke, and God answered him by a voice.
And when the voice of the horn waxed {{c1::louder}} and louder, Moses spoke, and God answered him by a voice.
And the LORD came down upon mount {{c1::Sinai}}, to the top of the mount; and the LORD called Moses to the top of the mount; and Moses went up.
And the LORD came down upon mount Sinai, to the top of the mount; and the LORD called {{c1::Moses}} to the top of the mount; and Moses went up.
And the LORD said unto {{c1::Moses}}: ‘Go down, charge the people, lest they break through unto the LORD to gaze, and many of them perish.
And the LORD said unto Moses: ‘{{c1::Go}} down, charge the people, lest they break through unto the LORD to gaze, and many of them perish.
And let the {{c1::priests}} also, that come near to the LORD, sanctify themselves, lest the LORD break forth upon them.’
And let the priests also, that come near to the LORD, {{c1::sanctify}} themselves, lest the LORD break forth upon them.’
And {{c1::Moses}} said unto the LORD: ‘The people cannot come up to mount Sinai; for thou didst charge us, saying: Set bounds about the mount, and sanctify it.’
And Moses said unto the LORD: ‘The people cannot come up to mount {{c1::Sinai}}; for thou didst charge us, saying: Set bounds about the mount, and sanctify it.’

And the LORD said unto him: ‘{{c1::Go}}, get thee down, and thou shalt come up, thou, and Aaron with thee; but let not the priests and the people break through to come up unto the LORD, lest He break forth upon them.’

And the LORD said unto him: ‘Go, get thee down, and thou shalt come up, thou, and {{c1::Aaron}} with thee; but let not the priests and the people break through to come up unto the LORD, lest He break forth upon them.’
So {{c1::Moses}} went down unto the people, and told them.
So Moses went down unto the {{c1::people}}, and told them.
And God spoke all these words, {{c1::saying}}:
I am the LORD thy God, who brought thee out of the land of {{c1::Egypt}}, out of the house of bondage.
I am the LORD thy God, who {{c1::brought}} thee out of the land of Egypt, out of the house of bondage.
Thou shalt have no other gods before {{c1::Me}}.
Thou shalt have no other gods {{c1::before}} Me.
Thou shalt not make unto thee a {{c1::graven}} image, nor any manner of likeness, of any thing that is in heaven above, or that is in the earth beneath, or that is in the water under the earth;
Thou shalt not make unto thee a graven image, nor any {{c1::manner}} of likeness, of any thing that is in heaven above, or that is in the earth beneath, or that is in the water under the earth;

thou shalt not bow down unto them, nor serve them; for {{c1::I}} the LORD thy God am a jealous God, visiting the iniquity of the fathers upon the children unto the third and fourth generation of them that hate Me;

thou shalt not bow down unto them, nor serve them; for I the LORD thy God am a jealous God, visiting the iniquity of the fathers upon the children unto the third and fourth generation of them that hate {{c1::Me}};
and showing mercy unto the thousandth generation of them that love {{c1::Me}} and keep My commandments.
and showing mercy unto the thousandth generation of them that love Me and keep {{c1::My}} commandments.
Thou shalt not take the name of the LORD thy God in vain; for the LORD will not hold him {{c1::guiltless}} that taketh His name in vain.
Thou shalt not take the name of the LORD thy God in vain; for the LORD will not hold him guiltless that {{c1::taketh}} His name in vain.
{{c1::Remember}} the sabbath day, to keep it holy.
Remember the {{c1::sabbath}} day, to keep it holy.
Six days shalt thou {{c1::labour}}, and do all thy work;

but the {{c1::seventh}} day is a sabbath unto the LORD thy God, in it thou shalt not do any manner of work, thou, nor thy son, nor thy daughter, nor thy man-servant, nor thy maid-servant, nor thy cattle, nor thy stranger that is within thy gates;

but the seventh day is a {{c1::sabbath}} unto the LORD thy God, in it thou shalt not do any manner of work, thou, nor thy son, nor thy daughter, nor thy man-servant, nor thy maid-servant, nor thy cattle, nor thy stranger that is within thy gates;
for in six days the LORD made {{c1::heaven}} and earth, the sea, and all that in them is, and rested on the seventh day; wherefore the LORD blessed the sabbath day, and hallowed it.
for in six days the LORD made heaven and earth, the sea, and all that in them is, and {{c1::rested}} on the seventh day; wherefore the LORD blessed the sabbath day, and hallowed it.
{{c1::Honour}} thy father and thy mother, that thy days may be long upon the land which the LORD thy God giveth thee.
Honour thy {{c1::father}} and thy mother, that thy days may be long upon the land which the LORD thy God giveth thee.
Thou shalt not {{c1::murder}}. Thou shalt not commit adultery. Thou shalt not steal. Thou shalt not bear false witness against thy neighbour.
Thou shalt not murder. Thou shalt not {{c1::commit}} adultery. Thou shalt not steal. Thou shalt not bear false witness against thy neighbour.

Thou shalt not covet thy {{c1::neighbour’s}} house; thou shalt not covet thy neighbour’s wife, nor his man-servant, nor his maid-servant, nor his ox, nor his ass, nor any thing that is thy neighbour’s.

Thou shalt not covet thy neighbour’s house; thou shalt not covet thy neighbour’s wife, nor his man-{{c1::servant}}, nor his maid-servant, nor his ox, nor his ass, nor any thing that is thy neighbour’s.
And all the {{c1::people}} perceived the thunderings, and the lightnings, and the voice of the horn, and the mountain smoking; and when the people saw it, they trembled, and stood afar off.
And all the people {{c1::perceived}} the thunderings, and the lightnings, and the voice of the horn, and the mountain smoking; and when the people saw it, they trembled, and stood afar off.
And they said unto {{c1::Moses}}: ‘Speak thou with us, and we will hear; but let not God speak with us, lest we die.’
And they said unto Moses: ‘{{c1::Speak}} thou with us, and we will hear; but let not God speak with us, lest we die.’
And {{c1::Moses}} said unto the people: ‘Fear not; for God is come to prove you, and that His fear may be before you, that ye sin not.’
And Moses said unto the people: ‘{{c1::Fear}} not; for God is come to prove you, and that His fear may be before you, that ye sin not.’
And the people stood afar off; but {{c1::Moses}} drew near unto the thick darkness where God was.
And the {{c1::people}} stood afar off; but Moses drew near unto the thick darkness where God was.
And the LORD said unto {{c1::Moses}}: Thus thou shalt say unto the children of Israel: Ye yourselves have seen that I have talked with you from heaven.
And the LORD said unto Moses: {{c1::Thus}} thou shalt say unto the children of Israel: Ye yourselves have seen that I have talked with you from heaven.
Ye shall not make with {{c1::Me}}—gods of silver, or gods of gold, ye shall not make unto you.
Ye shall not make with Me—gods of {{c1::silver}}, or gods of gold, ye shall not make unto you.

An altar of earth thou shalt make unto {{c1::Me}}, and shalt sacrifice thereon thy burnt-offerings, and thy peace-offerings, thy sheep, and thine oxen; in every place where I cause My name to be mentioned I will come unto thee and bless thee.

An altar of earth thou shalt make unto Me, and shalt sacrifice thereon thy burnt-offerings, and thy peace-offerings, thy sheep, and thine oxen; in every place where {{c1::I}} cause My name to be mentioned I will come unto thee and bless thee.
And if thou make {{c1::Me}} an altar of stone, thou shalt not build it of hewn stones; for if thou lift up thy tool upon it, thou hast profaned it.
And if thou make Me an altar of stone, thou shalt not build it of hewn {{c1::stones}}; for if thou lift up thy tool upon it, thou hast profaned it.
Neither shalt thou go up by steps unto {{c1::Mine}} altar, that thy nakedness be not uncovered thereon.
{{c1::Neither}} shalt thou go up by steps unto Mine altar, that thy nakedness be not uncovered thereon.
